notes: Parameter names mirror sequence.components.light_source.LightSource. Literature sources: Arenskoetter et al., Telecom Quantum Photonic Interface for a Ca-40 Single-Ion Quantum Memory, arXiv:2211.08841, and Zang et al., Simulation of Entanglement Generation between Absorptive Quantum Memories, arXiv:2212.08809. The current adapter target is a single-wavelength LightSource, so this resolved profile uses the channel-facing telecom arm of the Ca-40 SPDC/QFC source rather than claiming a full two-arm SPDC interface. Arenskoetter reports a generated pair rate 4.7e4 pairs/(s mW) at up to 20 mW, giving 9.4e5 Hz; 854-nm photons converted to the telecom C-band at 1550 nm; and a 250 MHz post-conversion filter stage. The bandwidth value converts 250 MHz FWHM at 1550 nm via Delta_lambda=lambda^2 Delta_nu/c and then divides by 2.355 to approximate the Gaussian sigma expected by LightSource. Zang supplies the SeQUeNCe/SPDC mean photon number mu=0.1. No cited source gives a stochastic phase-flip probability for this LightSource field, so phase_error=0.0 records a phase-controlled baseline rather than a claim of zero physical phase drift.
